meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Report details rising pressure on underground Catholics as China denies crackdown
items.summary: Human Rights Watch says China is increasing pressure on 12 million Catholics amid a decade-long campaign to align religion with party ideology.

items.title: South African ex-police chief gets tissue stuck on forehead while sweating during corruption inquiry
items.summary: A suspended South African police chief's testimony at the Madlanga Commission corruption inquiry was interrupted by a tissue stuck to his forehead.

items.title: Trump predicted Israel-Lebanon leaders would speak ‘tomorrow’ — Beirut shut it down as ceasefire emerges
items.summary: Lebanon's President Aoun will not speak with Netanyahu for now, a senior Lebanese official told Fox News Digital, dealing a setback to Trump's push for direct contact with Israel.

items.title: Russian missiles and drones bombard Ukraine in hourslong attack, killing at least 16
items.summary: Russia launched nearly 700 drones and dozens of missiles at Ukraine, killing at least 16 people and injuring over 80 in its biggest attack in weeks.

items.title: Hungary’s new leader once idolized Orbán — now he’s the man who brought him down
items.summary: Learn about Péter Magyar, the former Orbán insider who ended Hungary's 16-year ruling regime and is now set to lead the country into a new era.

items.title: Iran to execute first female protester tied to anti-regime unrest
items.summary: Iran prepares to execute its first female protester tied to the 2026 Tehran uprising as human rights groups warn of a deepening crackdown.

items.title: Allies rush thousands of drones to Ukraine as Russia unleashes deadly missile barrages
items.summary: Ukraine's allies pledge massive military aid, including 120,000 U.K. drones, as Russia launches 324 drones and ballistic missiles, officials report.

items.title: UN filing accuses UK of forced displacement as Diego Garcia tensions and security fears grow
items.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a crimes against humanity complaint at the U.N. over the alleged forced removal of Chagossian people from the Diego Garcia islands.

items.title: Nine dead, 13 wounded in second Turkish mass shooting in two days
items.summary: A 14-year-old boy allegedly shot and killed nine people at a Turkish middle school, the second school shooting in Turkey in just two days.

items.title: Iran shifts 20M barrels through ‘dark’ offshore oil network bypassing US port blockade, firm says
items.summary: Iran is moving roughly 20 million barrels of oil through covert offshore networks near Malaysia to bypass the U.S. blockade, Windward AI says.

items.title: Israeli strike on Hezbollah more devastating than 2024 pager attack, IDF says
items.summary: Israel says roughly 50 aircraft struck more than 100 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon in a devastating 60-second operation that killed 250 operatives.

items.title: From Auschwitz, Holocaust survivor issues urgent warning over rising antisemitism in Canada
items.summary: Holocaust survivor Nate Leipciger, 98, warns from Auschwitz about growing antisemitism in Canada, calling on the nation to stand up and take action now.

items.title: Synagogue in London targeted in attempted 'antisemitic hate crime,' UK police say
items.summary: Police in London are searching for two masked suspects behind an attempted arson attack on a synagogue that is now being treated as an "antisemitic hate crime."

items.title: Iran threatens to halt Red Sea traffic in response to US military blockade of ports
items.summary: Iran's military warns it could block exports and imports in the Red Sea, Persian Gulf and Sea of Oman if the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports is not lifted.

items.title: Russia's Lavrov says Iran has 'inalienable' right to enrich uranium, openly defying Trump's demands
items.summary: Russia's Sergey Lavrov says Iran has an inalienable right to enrich uranium for civilian purposes, pushing back against Trump's nuclear demands.

items.title: US stops 9 oil tankers attempting to break Trump's blockade on Iranian ports
items.summary: U.S. Central Command said Wednesday that nine ships have obeyed orders from American forces while attempting to break a blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: President Trump's negotiating team praised by nuclear experts for walking away from Pakistan talks
items.summary: Experts say the Trump administration was right to walk away from Iran nuclear talks after Tehran refused to agree to core demands on uranium enrichment.

items.title: Trump blasts close ally Meloni, says she’s failing US on Iran
items.summary: Trump publicly rebukes Italian PM Giorgia Meloni, accusing her of lacking courage on Iran, as Italy suspends its defense agreement with Israel.

items.title: Israel's spy chief says Iran mission will only end when 'extremist regime' is replaced
items.summary: Mossad Director Dadi Barnea reportedly said Israel's campaign against Iran will end only once the extremist regime in Tehran has been replaced.

items.title: Trump squeezed between Israel and Turkey as Netanyahu, Erdogan escalate feud
items.summary: Israel–Turkey tensions escalate sharply as Netanyahu and Erdogan clash over Gaza, Iran and regional influence, creating a diplomatic challenge for Trump.

items.title: 'Not on our watch': Global law enforcement leaders unite in Poland against hate
items.summary: Rising antisemitism since the Oct. 7 massacre drives a new global law enforcement effort focused on protecting vulnerable communities from hate crimes.

items.title: Zelenskyy announces 'the future is here' after war's first all-robot capture
items.summary: Ukraine captured a Russian position using only unmanned robots and drones, Zelenskyy says, calling it a first in the history of the war with Russia.

items.title: China slams US military blockade of Strait of Hormuz as a 'dangerous and irresponsible move'
items.summary: China slammed the U.S. military blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a "dangerous and irresponsible" move, urging all parties to honor a ceasefire agreement.

items.title: Gunman opens fire at high school in Turkey, wounding at least 16
items.summary: At least 16 people were hurt after a former student opened fire inside a high school in Siverek, Turkey, before killing himself, officials said.

items.title: Kim Jong Un oversees cruise missile launches from prized new North Korean warship
items.summary: State media claims Kim Jong Un watched cruise and anti-ship missile launches from North Korea's new warship Choe Hyon during Sunday's weapons tests.
